title: 1.1: Adds an invalidateQueriesOnSuccess parameter with default true to all 3 mutation factories (sitePluginDeactivateMutation, sitePluginAutoupdateDisableMutation, and sitePluginRemoveMutation) in packages/api-queries/src/site-plugins.ts

annotations.description: Each of the three mutation factories—sitePluginDeactivateMutation, sitePluginAutoupdateDisableMutation, and sitePluginRemoveMutation—must accept an invalidateQueriesOnSuccess boolean parameter that defaults to true. When true, plugin queries are invalidated on success (preserving current behavior). When false, query invalidation is skipped. Any implementation that achieves this behavioral contract is acceptable, such as conditionally calling the invalidation helper inside onSuccess, conditionally including the onSuccess property, or any other approach that satisfies the interface.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 50-61, 68-79, 86-97: Each of the three mutation factory signatures changes from () to ( invalidateQueriesOnSuccess = true ) and the onSuccess callback conditionally calls invalidatePluginsForSite.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'refactor the deactivate, autoupdate disable, and remove mutation factories so they natively support opting out of query invalidation' and 'the default behavior should stay the same for all existing callers.' The interface specifies Input: invalidateQueriesOnSuccess: boolean = true for all three factories. [3: Logical Necessity] Without this parameter, the delete modal cannot opt out of invalidation through the factory API. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must add this parameter to all three mutation factories with default true, so callers without arguments see no behavior change while the delete modal can pass false.

title: 1.2: Refactors the delete modal in client/dashboard/plugins/plugin/sites-with-this-plugin.tsx to use the mutation factories directly instead of spreading and overriding onSuccess

annotations.description: The delete modal's RenderModal callback in sites-with-this-plugin.tsx must be updated to call sitePluginDeactivateMutation, sitePluginAutoupdateDisableMutation, and sitePluginRemoveMutation with invalidation disabled (e.g. passing false), removing the previous pattern of spreading the mutation result and overriding onSuccess with an empty callback. The resulting useMutation calls should receive the factory return value directly without manual property overrides.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 20-38: The delete modal replaces { ...sitePluginDeactivateMutation(), onSuccess: () => {} } with sitePluginDeactivateMutation( false ), and similarly for the other two mutations.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'clean up the delete modal so it uses them directly without needing to spread and override anything' and describes the current pattern as 'spreading the mutation options and overriding onSuccess with an empty callback, which is fragile and unclear.' [3: Logical Necessity] Once the factories support opting out, the workaround in the delete modal becomes unnecessary and should be replaced with direct factory usage. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must update the delete modal to use the new factory parameter instead of the spread-and-override pattern.

title: 1.3: Renames the mutateAsync destructured variable from sitePluginUpdateMutation to a more specific alias in client/dashboard/plugins/plugin/sites-with-this-plugin.tsx

annotations.description: The mutateAsync variable destructured from useMutation( sitePluginUpdateMutation() ) must be renamed to a more descriptive alias (such as updateMutate or similar) that distinguishes it from the other mutation variables in the same component. The renamed variable must still be passed to buildBulkSitesPluginAction to preserve the updateAction functionality.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 9-12: const { mutateAsync } is changed to const { mutateAsync: updateMutate } and the subsequent buildBulkSitesPluginAction call uses updateMutate.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the mutateAsync variable from the update mutation in the same component should be given a more specific alias, since the component has several mutations and the generic name is confusing.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The component destructures mutateAsync from multiple useMutation calls (activate, deactivate, etc.) with specific aliases; the update mutation is the only one using the bare mutateAsync name. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must rename this variable to a more specific alias and update its usage in buildBulkSitesPluginAction accordingly.

title: 4.1: Modifies query invalidation logic for any of the 4 unmodified mutation factories (sitePluginActivateMutation, sitePluginUpdateMutation, sitePluginAutoupdateEnableMutation, and sitePluginInstallMutation) in packages/api-queries/src/site-plugins.ts

annotations.description: Only sitePluginDeactivateMutation, sitePluginAutoupdateDisableMutation, and sitePluginRemoveMutation should have their invalidation behavior modified. The remaining four mutation factories—sitePluginActivateMutation, sitePluginUpdateMutation, sitePluginAutoupdateEnableMutation, and sitePluginInstallMutation—must remain unchanged.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch only modifies sitePluginDeactivateMutation (line 51), sitePluginAutoupdateDisableMutation (line 69), and sitePluginRemoveMutation (line 87). The activate, update, autoupdateEnable, and install mutations are untouched.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt specifies 'refactor the deactivate, autoupdate disable, and remove mutation factories,' explicitly naming only three. [4: Qualitative Requirement] Modifying unrelated mutations introduces unnecessary scope creep and risk. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must leave other mutation factories' invalidation logic unchanged.

title: 4.2: Modifies testing logic in the codebase

annotations.description: The agent must not modify any testing logic. All test changes have already been handled separately. Any edits to test files, test utilities, test configurations, or test assertions constitute a violation.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch does not modify any test files. The prompt explicitly states 'Do NOT modify any testing logic in any way.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'I've already taken care of all changes to the test files. Do NOT modify any testing logic in any way. Your task is to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only.' [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must leave all test files and testing logic untouched.
